basic_details.job_title: BHEL Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for Part Time Medical Consultant Posts
basic_details.post_name: Part Time Medical Consultant (General Duty Medical Officer-GDMO) - MBBS
basic_details.short_information: Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL) Part Time Medical Consultant (General Duty Medical Officer-GDMO) - MBBS Notification 2026 has been released on 04 May 2026 on the official website https://pser.bhel.com/ . A total of 3 vacancies have been announced through the BHEL Part Time Medical Consultant (General Duty Medical Officer-GDMO) - MBBS Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 65 years. Age relaxation up to 70 years may be considered with specific approval from the Competent Authority.

qualification.education_qualification: MBBS Degree recognized by the Medical Council of India
qualification.details: MBBS Degree recognized by the Medical Council of India. Registration Certificate for MBBS issued by Medical Council of India or State Medical Council. Retired Medical Professional from BHEL / Central Govt. Organization / other CPSEs.

vacancy_details.Post Name: PTMC-01 (General Duty Medical Officer-GDMO - MBBS, 3 Hours Morning)
PTMC-02 (General Duty Medical Officer-GDMO - MBBS, 3 Hours Evening)
PTMC-03 (General Duty Medical Officer-GDMO - MBBS, 6 Hours)

how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for BHEL Various Posts Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: https://pser.bhel.com/.
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 19-05-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the BHEL Part Time Medical Consultant posts?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the offline application for the BHEL Part Time Medical Consultant posts is 19th May 2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess an MBBS Degree recognized by the Medical Council of India and a valid Registration Certificate. Retired medical professionals from government organizations are also eligible.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are available for Part Time Medical Consultant posts?
faq.answer: There are a total of 3 vacancies for the Part Time Medical Consultant (General Duty Medical Officer-GDMO) - MBBS posts.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary offered for these positions?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will be paid Rs. 500/- per hour for their services as Part Time Medical Consultants.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for applying to these posts?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for applicants is 65 years. However, this can be relaxed up to 70 years with specific approval from the competent authority.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for BHEL recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) in the official notification.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for BHEL Part Time Medical Consultant posts?
faq.answer: The selection of candidates will be based on a Personal Interview conducted by BHEL.

faq.question: Q8. In which mode should the application be submitted?
faq.answer: The application process is strictly offline. Candidates need to download the form, fill it, and submit it via Speed Post or Registered Post.

faq.question: Q9. Where is the job location for these BHEL posts?
faq.answer: The job location for these Part Time Medical Consultant posts is Kolkata, West Bengal.

faq.question: Q10. Can a candidate who is currently working apply for this post?
faq.answer: The eligibility criteria mention retired medical professionals. Therefore, candidates who are currently employed may need to check the specific terms and conditions in the official notification regarding their current employment status.
